name: Euro Area
climate: Temperate maritime to Mediterranean, continental interior

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Eurozone is a subset of the EU; non-euro EU members (Poland, Sweden, etc.) are key trade partners

neighbors.name: Nordic Council
neighbors.relevance: Finland is a eurozone member; Denmark pegs to the euro; Sweden trades heavily with the zone

neighbors.name: Visegrád Group
neighbors.relevance: Slovakia is a eurozone member; Poland, Czechia, Hungary are deeply integrated via supply chains

neighbors.name: Mediterranean Sea Basin
neighbors.relevance: Southern eurozone members (Spain, Italy, Greece) face Mediterranean-specific risks

neighbors.name: NAFTA/USMCA
neighbors.relevance: USD-EUR exchange rate dynamics; transatlantic capital flows and trade

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Energy import dependency; sanctions impact on eurozone banks and trade

links.description: All eurozone members are EU members; single market integration amplifies monetary union
German-Central European automotive supply chain is the eurozone's industrial backbone
Germany's industrial heartland drives eurozone manufacturing output and exports
Southern eurozone members depend on Mediterranean shipping lanes for energy and trade
ECB coordinates with G7 central banks on monetary policy and financial stability
Chinese investment in eurozone ports and infrastructure raises strategic dependency concerns
